rulings.Case Title: HOLLYWOOD PAWS FRANCHISING, LLC, A CALIFORNIA VS. LORELLE POUNCEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Strike Pltf'S Complaint As A Strategic Lawsuit Against Public Participation (Slapp); Request For Judicial Notice; Index Of Parties And Initials And Acronyms Used As References In The Anti-Slapp Motion
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JANUARY 25, 2008 LINE 9. DEFENDANT LORELLE POUNCEY'S Motion To Strike PLAINTIFF'S Complaint As A Strategic Lawsuit Against Public Participation (SLAPP); (Request For Judicial Notice): Hearing required on the issue of plaintiff prevailing on its claim. The record reflects that plaintiff has obtained a favorable ruling of the underlying case and that plaintiff can present evidence of malice. The Court finds that certain critical testimony offered by plaintiff has not been effectively authenticated and has sustained defendant's objections 25 through 28. Does that ruling effectively defeat plaintiff's prima facie showing? Assuming this evidence is admissible, do the following facts provide a basis for a reasonable attorney to believe that a tenable claim could be made as to plaintiff: the solicitation of Pouncey to a Franchising LLC event; shared office space with Paws LLC; the relationships between and among individuals holding positions in both entities; Lawerence Lionetti's role in both entities; the fact that Paws LLC is a franchisee of Franchising LLC; and both entities allegedly being involved in vocational training/animal acting workshops? =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: JACQUELINE PIERI VS. CHRISTOPHER DOLAN et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JANUARY 25, 2008 LINE 10. CROSS-DEFENDANT ROCK PLICHCIK'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT is continued to FEBRUARY 14, 2008. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: JACQUELINE PIERI VS. CHRISTOPHER DOLAN et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JANUARY 25, 2008 LINE 10. CROSS-DEFENDANT ROCK PLICHCIK'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS-COMPLAINT In the lengthy preamble to the cross-complaints causes of action, cross-complainants refer to plaintiff's Notice of Withdrawal of the subject property under the Ellis Act and related acts ( 34, 58, 78, 79, 107, 111, 127 and 129-134), the commencement of unlawful detainer proceedings ( 37, 100, 128), seeking damages for cross-complainant's alleged wrongful holding over ( 40, 41 and 126) and seeking declaratory relief related to a relocation payment (132 and 13). At the same time, cross-complaint alleges that the "motivating factor in Pieri's decision to attempt to evict the Cross-complainants were the complaint made by Cross-complainants concerning the conditions of the building as raised in the December 13, 2003 letter." ( 87, 102 "motivated in part" and 123 "constructively evicted" based on the conditions in the building.) In deciding the motion, the Court looks to the graveman of the complaint. The motion is denied as to the following causes of action: 1, 3, 4, 5, 6 and 8. Each of these causes of action center on habitability and/or maintenance of the premises. The fact that defendant incorporates other allegations does not change the substance of these claims. The 2nd, 9th, 11th and 12th causes of action rely on both protected and unprotected activity. In responding to the motion, cross-complainants rely on the Dolan Declaration filed in opposition to a prior motion in this case. The Court does not have a copy of the declaration. Accordingly, cross-complainants are directed to file a courtesy copy with the Court by January 28, and the hearing is continued to CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 14, 2004. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
